seoTitle: View All Invoices in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to filter and audit paid, unpaid, open, and recurring invoices in QuickBooks Online from one centralized dashboard.
intro: Tracking cash flow and outstanding balances gets much easier once you know how to filter your full invoice history in one place, instead of digging through individual customer pages. This guide covers the centralized invoice dashboard, filtering by status, and managing recurring billing templates.

steps.heading: Open the invoices dashboard
steps.body: Click All Apps or Sales, then select the Invoices tab to reach your full transaction list.

steps.heading: Review the summary tiles
steps.body: Check the high-contrast money bar at the top for combined totals of open, overdue, and paid balances.

steps.heading: Filter by status
steps.body: Click the Unpaid or Open header tiles to instantly filter the grid down to just outstanding balances.

steps.heading: Sort by date or customer
steps.body: Use the date funnel selector to sort active invoices by month or by customer name.

steps.heading: Check recurring invoices
steps.body: Go to Accounting, then Recurring Transactions, filter by Invoice type, and review your list of active automated billings.

faq.question: How do I quickly see just the invoices that are overdue?
faq.answer: Click the Unpaid or Open filter tile at the top of the invoice dashboard to narrow the grid to only outstanding balances.

faq.question: Can I mark an invoice as paid directly from this list view?
faq.answer: Yes, clicking Receive Payment directly from the list view lets you update an invoice's balance without opening it separately.

faq.question: Where do I manage recurring invoice templates?
faq.answer: Go to Accounting, then Recurring Transactions, and filter by Invoice type to see and edit active automated billings.

faq.question: Can I sort invoices by a specific customer?
faq.answer: Yes, the date funnel selector on the main invoice grid also lets you sort by customer name.
